articles.title: Customers slowly handing more power to AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon
articles.summary: The article discusses how AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon are disrupting the traditional broadband duopoly previously held by cable and DSL companies. Through Fixed Wireless Access (FWA) services, the "Big Three" carriers are rapidly gaining market share, offering more choices, lower rates, and greater availability to customers. This shift, however, raises questions about whether power is simply transferring from one set of large corporations to another.

articles.summary: Delta Air Lines is set to launch its first-ever twice-daily flights to Santa Rosa, California, from Salt Lake City in October, using SkyWest Embraer E175 aircraft. This move directly competes with Alaska Airlines, which will also begin flights to Salt Lake City from Santa Rosa in November. The increased service reflects growing interest in Santa Rosa Charles M. Schulz–Sonoma County Airport, which is projected to see a 38% increase in departures this year.

articles.content: Thermo Fisher Scientific recently held its 2026 Investor Day, where it affirmed its quarterly dividend and provided updates on AI integration, capital deployment, and portfolio initiatives, including the US$9.00 billion Clario acquisition. The company is emphasizing its AI-enabled clinical and laboratory solutions and expanded bioanalytical capacity to support evolving pharma and biotech workflows. While the updates don't materially change the existing investment narrative, they reinforce the company's focus on technological deepening and disciplined capital deployment, alongside the persistent risk of integrating large acquisitions.

articles.title: Does Amgen’s CFO Transition and Board Structure Shape a New Governance Story for AMGN?
articles.summary: Amgen's CFO Peter Griffith is retiring, with Thomas Dittrich rejoining as the new CFO on September 1, 2026. Simultaneously, shareholders rejected a proposal for an independent board chair, highlighting a focus on leadership continuity versus oversight. articles.title: PH to benefit from BlackRock, IFC-backed infra credit fund
articles.summary: BlackRock Inc., the world's largest money manager, will invest in Southeast Asia, including the Philippines, through its Emerging Asia Infrastructure Credit Fund. This fund, backed by the International Finance Corp. (IFC) with an investment of up to $50 million, aims to finance industrial and infrastructure projects to support the region's transition to a low-carbon economy. With a target size of $750 million, the fund will primarily focus on Southeast Asia, channeling 80 percent of its capital commitments to countries like the Philippines, Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam, Laos, and Cam
articles.content: BlackRock Inc., the world's largest money manager, will invest in Southeast Asia, including the Philippines, through its Emerging Asia Infrastructure Credit Fund. This fund, backed by the International Finance Corp. (IFC) with an investment of up to $50 million, aims to finance industrial and infrastructure projects to support the region's transition to a low-carbon economy. With a target size of $750 million, the fund will primarily focus on Southeast Asia, channeling 80 percent of its capital commitments to countries like the Philippines, Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia, while also allocating up to 20 percent to South Asia.

articles.summary: Dyne Therapeutics has launched its Phase 3 FORZETTO trial for DYNE-251 to treat Duchenne muscular dystrophy, aiming for traditional marketing approval after a potential U.S. Accelerated Approval. This clinical advancement reinforces Dyne's registrational path and has positively impacted its stock, despite the company's current unprofitability and reliance on external capital. The trial is a critical step, but also raises the stakes, as disappointing results could quickly alter the company's investment narrative and investor expectations.

articles.title: Tesla recalls more than 14,000 Model Y vehicles over label issue
articles.summary: Tesla is recalling 14,575 Model Y vehicles from 2025 and 2026 due to an inconsistent automated vision-scanning tool that failed to verify certification labels, which provide crucial weight specifications. The absence of these labels could increase crash risks if vehicles are overloaded. The company fixed the tool in its Fremont and Austin factories after discovering the issue and is not aware of any related crashes or injuries.

Despite the selloff, a valuation model suggests NEE is undervalued with a target price around $110, implying a 25% upside due to expected revenue growth, strong operating margins, and continued premium valuation in the utilities sector. articles.summary: Match Group’s refreshed analyst narrative comes with a higher modeled fair value, rising from about US$36.29 to about US$41.06. That change aligns with mixed analyst views, as some firms are raising price targets on improving product execution while others are trimming targets or remaining cautious until user and revenue trends more clearly reflect those efforts. Read on to see what is driving these differing perspectives and how you can monitor the next phase of this evolving story. Analyst...

articles.title: Return on equity % of The Bank Of New York Mellon Corporation Depositary Shares, each representing a 1/4,000th interest in a share of Series K Noncumulative Perpetual Preferred Stoc – BOATS:BNY/PK
articles.summary: This article displays the return on equity percentage for The Bank Of New York Mellon Corporation Depositary Shares, specifically those representing a 1/4,000th interest in a share of Series K Noncumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock (BNY/PK). It provides a snapshot of this financial metric, indicating the company's profitability. The page is part of a TradingView financial overview for the BNY/PK security.

articles.title: GM, Ford, Stellantis CEOs go back to the drawing board with EVs
articles.summary: Unless your name is Tesla, the U.S. EV market has been a minefield for original equipment manufacturers. There were dangerous cracks even as customers set a record sales pace through the first three quarters of 2025. U.S. consumers flocked to dealerships to buy EVs last year, right up until ...

articles.title: What CEO Sanjay Mehrota Just Said Shows Why Micron Is About to Become a $1,000 Stock
articles.summary: The AI boom was supposed to slow down by now. At least that was the theory. Data centers are straining electric grids, utilities are warning about power shortages, copper supplies are tightening, and semiconductor packaging capacity remains constrained. Yet demand for AI infrastructure keeps accelerating anyway. That’s because the AI race has shifted from experimentation ...
